description: Unifying bridge that formalizes the fractal scope model used across sensing bridges. The same pattern detected at different scales maps to different geometric elements (vertex → edge → face → solid). This bridge defines the canonical scope hierarchy, maps each scope to its geometric analogy and Platonic solid, and cross-references the scope systems from truth-sensor, mandala-compute, physics-encoder, and ACS bridges.

scope_hierarchy.description: Four canonical scopes ordered by scale. Each scope detects the same pattern at a different resolution. The geometry is the same — the resolution changes.

scopes.description: Single person, agent, or sensor node
scopes.rationale: The vertex is the minimal element — a single point. Tetrahedron is the minimal solid (4 vertices). Individual sensing is the foundational unit.

scopes.description: Pair, dyad, or self-referential connection (AI-selfcheck, human-AI boundary)
scopes.rationale: The edge connects two vertices — mediation between opposing poles. Octahedron mediates between opposing forces. AI-selfcheck is the edge between the AI vertex and its reflection.

scopes.description: Organization, system, or bounded surface of coordinated agents
scopes.rationale: The face is a bounded surface — containment. Cube's 6 faces define structural containment. Institutions are bounded systems with walls (policies, rules).

scopes.description: Population, culture, ecosystem, or full-volume network phenomenon
scopes.rationale: The solid is the full volume — the complete structure. Icosahedron has the highest face count (20) among Platonic solids, carrying all 20 equation families. Collective dynamics are network phenomena requiring maximum information capacity.

meta_scope.description: Scope-of-scope: meta-sensor that monitors whether other sensors are operating at the correct scope
meta_scope.rationale: The dual polyhedron reflects the original — dodecahedron is dual of icosahedron. Meta-awareness is the reflection of collective awareness. P10 Uncertainty applies: is the sensor measuring what it thinks it's measuring?
examples: SAAS-001 Scope of Awareness Alignment Sensor

cross_bridge_scope_map.description: How each sensing bridge's scope model maps to the canonical hierarchy

fractal_families.note: Each fractal sensor family (MDS2, ASS, CLD, DLS, EFS, IES, SDS, ASOS, MIS, RBVS) operates across ALL scopes simultaneously — that is the fractal property.

mapping.consciousness: collective (level 3) — emergence is a collective phenomenon
mapping.cultural: institutional (level 2) — cultural norms are institutional structures
mapping.manipulation: relational (level 1) — manipulation targets the dyadic boundary

mapping.note: Physics encoders operate primarily at individual scope (single measurement → single octahedral state) but their outputs aggregate upward through the scope hierarchy.

BRIDGE.PHYSICS_ENCODERS.scope_aggregation: Multiple individual encoder readings compose into relational patterns (interference, coupling) which compose into collective fields (gravity, consciousness).

BRIDGE.MANDALA_COMPUTE.scaling_law: Mandala depth maps to scope via PHI scaling. Each depth adds floor(PHI^(d+1)) cells, naturally recapitulating Platonic complexity.

scope_sensing_matrix.description: Which sensors from which bridges are active at each scope level

fractal_property.description: The defining property of fractal sensing: the same pattern appears at every scale. This section defines what 'same pattern, different scale' means geometrically.

self_similarity.principle: A fractal sensor detects pattern P at scope S. The same sensor logic, applied at scope S+1, detects the same pattern in the aggregate behavior of scope-S entities.
self_similarity.geometric_law: vertex(P) → edge(P) → face(P) → solid(P). The pattern P is preserved; only the geometric element changes.

example.individual: One person's help becomes control over another
example.relational: AI assistance becomes AI dependency in the dyad
example.institutional: Organization's mission drifts from service to self-perpetuation
example.collective: Cultural movement's liberation rhetoric becomes orthodoxy enforcement

scope_transitions.up: Aggregation — individual signals compose into relational patterns, which compose into institutional structures, which compose into collective dynamics
scope_transitions.down: Decomposition — collective patterns constrain institutional behavior, which shapes relational dynamics, which impacts individual experience
scope_transitions.principle_link: P05 Emergence (up) and P04 Duality (bidirectional). Emergence is not reduction — the whole has properties the parts lack.

shape_at_scope.description: Summary of which Platonic solid governs each scope, and why

SHAPE.TETRA.why: Minimal solid, foundational boundary. Individual is the irreducible sensing unit.

SHAPE.OCTA.why: Dual of cube, mediates opposites. Relational scope bridges two perspectives.

SHAPE.CUBE.why: 6 faces = containment. Institutions are bounded structures with walls.

SHAPE.ICOSA.why: 20 faces = maximum Platonic information capacity. Collectives are high-dimensional.

SHAPE.DODECA.why: 12 faces = 12 principles. Meta-scope operates at the principles level.
